story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued directions for the implementation of the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme for various entities, including Commercial Banks, Regional Rural Banks, Rural Co-operative Banks, and Small Finance Banks. These directions aim to standardize the KCC Scheme across different types of banks in India. The RBI has issued separate directions for each type of bank to ensure uniformity in the implementation of the scheme.

story_clusters.summary: India has increased its oil imports from Russia and the UAE in June, ahead of the reopening of the Hormuz Strait. This surge in imports is attributed to India's efforts to hedge its energy needs ahead of the Hormuz reopening.

articles.title: NRE Account Credits Cannot Be Taxed Without Proof They Are Unexplained: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The ITAT in Ahmedabad deleted an addition relating to an NRE account after finding that the credits were linked to overseas salary remittances and lacked evidence of unexplained income. This decision impacts taxation in India and highlights the importance of proper documentation. The ITAT also remanded the larger addition for fresh verification due to inadequate SFT reconciliation.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT deleted an addition relating to an NRE account after finding that the credits were linked to overseas salary remittances and lacked evidence of unexplained income. It remanded the larger addition for fresh verification due to inadequate SFT reconciliation. The post NRE Account Credits Cannot Be Taxed Without Proof They Are Unexplained: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: FMV of Surrendered Tenancy Rights Is Cost of Acquisition for Redeveloped Property: ITAT Mumbai
articles.summary: The Mumbai ITAT held that ownership premises received under a redevelopment scheme are acquired in exchange for valuable tenancy rights and cannot be treated as having a nil cost. It directed the Assessing Officer to adopt the fair market value of the surrendered tenancy rights for recomputing capital gains. articles.summary: The Bombay High Court recently, while quashing a rape FIR, ordered the Director General of Police (DGP), Maharashtra to circulate the details of the complainant woman to all the police stations across the State so that the police exercises caution in future on receiving any complaint from the said woman as she is "habitual" in filing false rape, domestic violence and cruelty cases. articles.title: Failure To Check Police Brutality Could Make It 'Like Nazi Germany': Patna HC Orders FIR Against SHO For Alleged Custodial Torture
articles.summary: The Patna High Court has directed registration of an FIR against a Station House Officer accused of brutally assaulting a man and allegedly breaking both his legs, holding that referring the victim to the Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC at this stage would amount to “further injustice” and warning that unchecked police brutality could make the national police “like one of Nazi Germany.” A Single Judge Bench of Justice Jitendra Kumar was hearing a criminal writ petition seeking registration

articles.title: Reassessment Quashed as Third-Party Loose Papers Had No Live Link with Assessee: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court held that reassessment under Section 148 cannot be based on third-party loose papers that fail to establish a live link with the assessee. The Court quashed the notice as the material was vague and unrelated to the petitioner's transaction. The post Reassessment Quashed as Third-Party Loose Papers Had No Live Link with Assessee: Gujarat HC appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The Gujarat High Court held that reassessment under Section 148 cannot be based on third-party loose papers that fail to establish a live link with the assessee. The Court quashed the notice as the material was vague and unrelated to the petitioner's transaction. The post Reassessment Quashed as Third-Party Loose Papers Had No Live Link with Assessee: Gujarat HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NCLT Orders Rectification of Ambuja Cements Share Register Due to Fraudulent Transfer
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) in Ahmedabad ordered the rectification of Ambuja Cements' share register due to a fraudulent transfer. The tribunal held that the transferee failed to establish any lawful title to 21,000 equity shares. This decision aims to restore the original shareholders' names and rectify the company's Register of Members.
articles.raw_summary: NCLT Ahmedabad held that the transferee failed to establish any lawful title to 21,000 equity shares, making the transfer unsustainable. The Tribunal directed restoration of the original shareholders' names and rectification of the company's Register of Members. The post NCLT Orders Rectification of Ambuja Cements Share Register Due to Fraudulent Transfer appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment Notices as Sanction Was Obtained Under Wrong Provision
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court held that sanction under Section 151(i) instead of Section 151(ii) for AYs 2016-17 and 2017-18 rendered reassessment notices invalid. It also quashed the consequential assessment orders and demand notices. The post Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment Notices as Sanction Was Obtained Under Wrong Provision appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The Bombay High Court held that sanction under Section 151(i) instead of Section 151(ii) for AYs 2016-17 and 2017-18 rendered reassessment notices invalid. It also quashed the consequential assessment orders and demand notices. The post Bombay HC Quashes Reassessment Notices as Sanction Was Obtained Under Wrong Provision appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC Upholds Dismissal as Employee Had No Right to Representative from Another Bank’s Union
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the dismissal of an employee who was denied representation by a union official from another bank. The court ruled that the Service Code permits representation only through a union or association of the Bank of Cochin's employees or with permission by a lawyer. This decision has significant implications for employment law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that the Service Code permitted representation only through a union or association of the Bank of Cochin's employees or, with permission, by a lawyer. Denial of representation by an office-bearer of another bank's union did not invalidate the disciplinary proceedings. The post SC Upholds Dismissal as Employee Had No Right to Representative from Another Bank s Union appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: IRDAI Proposes Major Actuarial Rule Changes to Align with Insurance Law Amendments
articles.summary: IRDAI has proposed major changes to actuarial rules to align with the Insurance Law Amendments. This move aims to overhaul actuarial governance, reporting, investment norms, and insurer compliance. The changes are expected to impact the insurance sector in India.
articles.raw_summary: IRDAI's draft 2026 amendments overhaul actuarial governance, reporting, investment norms, and insurer compliance to align with the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025. The post IRDAI Proposes Major Actuarial Rule Changes to Align with Insurance Law Amendments appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 69C Addition Deleted as Diary Entries Alone cannot Prove Unexplained Expenditure
articles.summary: ITAT Chennai held that loose sheets and estimates alone cannot justify an addition under Section 69B without independent corroborative evidence. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of investment outside the books of account. articles.title: ITAT Deletes Unexplained Investment Addition as Investments Were Already in Balance Sheet
articles.summary: The ITAT Nagpur deleted an addition of unexplained investments after finding that the Assessing Officer had added disclosed investments. This decision affects taxpayers in India. The ITAT's ruling is a significant development in tax law.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Nagpur held that investments already disclosed in the balance sheet could not be treated as unexplained investments. It deleted the addition after finding that the Assessing Officer had added disclosed investments. The scheme aims to identify a lead bank for each district to coordinate banking activities. This initiative is expected to enhance financial inclusion and access to banking services for rural communities.

articles.title: RBI issues Circular on Lead Bank Scheme
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India had on February 13, 2026, issued the draft Circular on the revised guidelines of the Lead Bank Scheme, seeking feedback from the public. 2. articles.title: Internet now a lifeline, focus should shift from popularising access to protecting users: IT Secretary
articles.summary: The Indian government is shifting its focus from promoting internet access to protecting users and ensuring robust internet infrastructure. IT Secretary S Krishnan emphasized the need for a multi-stakeholder approach to internet governance. This move aims to ensure the safety and security of internet users in India.
articles.raw_summary: The government's digital policy is shifting from promoting access to actively protecting users and ensuring robust internet infrastructure, IT Secretary S Krishnan announced. He emphasised the need for a multi-stakeholder approach to internet governance, involving academia, users, and private firms.

articles.title: India, US to hold ministerial-level talks on bilateral trade pact this week
articles.summary: In a significant development, India and the US are set to conclude the first phase of their trade agreement this week. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is scheduled to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, with discussions geared towards finalizing an interim deal before the July 24 deadline, coinciding with the expiration of US temporary tariffs.
articles.raw_summary: In a significant development, India and the US are set to conclude the first phase of their trade agreement this week. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is scheduled to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, with discussions geared towards finalizing an interim deal before the July 24 deadline, coinciding with the expiration of US temporary tariffs.

articles.title: Exports rise 15 per cent in Apr-Jun 14 despite global uncertainties, says Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports surged approximately 15% in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He highlighted robust growth despite significant US tariffs, with May exports reaching a six-month high. Goyal also urged chartered accountants to contribute to a 'Viksit Bharat,' emphasizing local cleanliness and ethical business practices as foundational steps.
articles.raw_summary: India's merchandise exports surged approximately 15% in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He highlighted robust growth despite significant US tariffs, with May exports reaching a six-month high. Goyal also urged chartered accountants to contribute to a 'Viksit Bharat,' emphasizing local cleanliness and ethical business practices as foundational steps.

articles.title: India initiates anti-dumping probe against a Chinese, Japanese chemical used in tyre, rubber items
articles.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into Resorcinol imports from China and Japan. This action follows a complaint alleging that cheap imports are harming the domestic industry. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies will determine if dumping has occurred and caused injury. If confirmed, duties may be recommended to ensure fair trade practices for Indian producers.
articles.raw_summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into Resorcinol imports from China and Japan. This action follows a complaint alleging that cheap imports are harming the domestic industry. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies will determine if dumping has occurred and caused injury. If confirmed, duties may be recommended to ensure fair trade practices for Indian producers.

articles.title: India, US 'very close' to inking a trade deal, says Trump after meet with Modi
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that India and the US are very close to finalizing a trade deal, following a meeting with Prime Minister Narendra Modi on the sidelines of the G7 summit. Modi raised concerns about the safety of Indian seafarers in the Gulf, emphasizing their security and freedom of navigation.

articles.title: Bringing back open-market buybacks through stock exchanges is the right decision
articles.summary: SEBI’s decision to restore open-market buybacks through stock exchanges marks a pragmatic regulatory shift that supports efficient capital allocation. The move recognises evolving market dynamics while maintaining safeguards, giving companies greater flexibility to return surplus capital and strengthening confidence in market-led decision-making.

articles.title: India's data centre pipeline hits 8.33 GW as AI demand reshapes digital infra: Knight Frank India
articles.summary: India's data centre sector is witnessing an unprecedented infrastructure build-up, with the total development pipeline across major markets reaching 8.33 GW, according to Knight Frank India. The scale of future supply, driven by accelerating artificial intelligence adoption, cloud computing growth and data localisation requirements, is more than five times the country's current live data centre capacity of 1.6 GW.

articles.title: From mountains to plains to high seas, Indian forces take part in yoga sessions pan-India; Rajnath performs yoga with 1,000 soldiers in Shillong, calls it ‘holistic science’
articles.summary: Across India and its waters, armed forces personnel marked the 12th International Day of Yoga with widespread sessions. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh led a large gathering in Shillong, emphasizing yoga's role in holistic well-being and a disciplined life. From high-altitude posts to parade grounds, soldiers, sailors, and air warriors embraced the theme 'Yoga for Healthy Ageing,' showcasing India's ancient traditions as a global movement for humanity's health.

articles.title: Modi commissions 3 indigenous frontline naval ships, says ‘strong maritime capability deciding factor for country's eco, strategic influence’
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi commissioned three indigenous naval platforms—INS Dunagiri, INS Sanshodhak, and INS Agray—in Kolkata, marking a significant stride in India's self-reliance in defence manufacturing. These advanced vessels bolster the nation's maritime security and operational capabilities. The event underscores India's growing prowess in shipbuilding and its commitment to becoming a major global defence producer, with over 40 indigenously built warships inducted recently.

articles.title: Sumit Roy, key aide of TMC's Abhishek Banerjee, booked in fraud and forgery case
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee's close aide, Sumit Roy, has been booked in a fraud and forgery case in West Bengal. Roy is currently absconding and a lookout notice has been issued. This development comes after the party's recent electoral setback in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Trinamool Congress national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee's close aide, Sumit Roy, faces fraud and forgery charges in West Bengal. Roy, who is currently absconding, has also been implicated in a separate land-grab case, leading to a lookout notice and searches at Banerjee's residence. The developments follow the party's recent electoral setback in the state where it lost power after 15 years.

articles.title: ADB to maintain private sector funding pace in India, eyes $1 billion support in 2026
articles.summary: India continues to be the Asian Development Bank's top market for private sector investments. The ADB plans to inject approximately $1 billion in direct financing this year, aligning with India's development goals. This follows a significant $2 billion channeled into India's private sector last year. The bank will maintain this momentum, focusing on green energy, e-mobility, and urban infrastructure, while also boosting trade and supply chain finance.

articles.title: India ramps up Russian, UAE oil purchases as refiners hedge ahead of Hormuz reopening
articles.summary: India's crude oil imports from Russia surged in June, solidifying Moscow's position as the top supplier, while UAE shipments remained strong. This strategic diversification aims to secure energy supplies amidst ongoing uncertainties surrounding the Strait of Hormuz. Despite a gradual reopening of the strait, refiners are maintaining robust purchases from alternative sources, indicating a lasting shift in India's energy sourcing strategy.

articles.title: Delhi High Court rejects Telegram's appeal against temporary blocking of app
articles.summary: A New Delhi court has rejected Telegram's appeal against a temporary ban in India. The ban was imposed to curb alleged exam-related fraud. India's IT ministry cited concerns about channels offering exam questions for sale. The court ruled that government orders banning the app were reasoned and followed legal procedures.

articles.title: RBI MPC minutes: Policy misstep fears drove rate pause amid West Asia uncertainty
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has opted for a cautious "wait and watch" stance, holding the repo rate steady due to significant geopolitical and weather-related uncertainties. Concerns over rising crude oil prices and potential El Nino impacts are driving inflation worries. While core inflation remains subdued, the committee emphasized vigilance against generalized price increases, prioritizing data dependency before considering any policy rate adjustments.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has opted for a cautious "wait and watch" stance, holding the repo rate steady due to significant geopolitical and weather-related uncertainties. Concerns over rising crude oil prices and potential El Nino impacts are driving inflation worries. While core inflation remains subdued, the committee emphasized vigilance against generalized price increases, prioritizing data dependency before considering any policy rate adjustments.

articles.title: RBI MPC minutes: 'Energy prices unlikely to return to pre-conflict levels,' says economist Saugata Bhattacharya
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. External member Saugata Bhattacharya highlighted escalating inflationary risks from supply shocks, weather patterns, and energy prices. He also noted rising inflation expectations and potential global spillovers. Despite these concerns, he advocated for the status quo, citing tight domestic financial conditions and the need for a risk management approach amid prevailing uncertainties.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. He also noted rising inflation expectations and potential global spillovers. Despite these concerns, he advocated for the status quo, citing tight domestic financial conditions and the need for a risk management approach amid prevailing uncertainties.

articles.title: RBI expected to hold repo at 5.25% as uncertainty eases, will watch food and fuel pass-through: BofA Securities
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its wait-and-watch stance and keep the repo rate at 5.25 per cent in coming meetings as the US-Iran peace agreement reduces geopolitical uncertainty, while it monitors food and fuel price pass-through before acting, BofA Securities said in a note on the June MPC minutes.

articles.title: NSE's Rs 30,000 crore IPO set to spotlight exchange's dominance in Indian markets, dependence on options trading: Zerodha analysis

articles.title: Why marine insurance premiums may not come down despite easing Strait of Hormuz tensions
articles.summary: Marine insurance premiums may not decrease despite easing tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. This is because there is no formal guarantee of stability on the ground. The situation may continue to affect insurance pricing.
articles.raw_summary: For now, despite indications of possible de-escalation, the absence of formal guarantees and on-ground stability is expected to keep marine insurance pricing anchored at higher levels.

articles.title: Gameskraft founders' ED arrest illegal, Karnataka High Court orders immediate release
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has ruled that the Enforcement Directorate’s arrest of Gameskraft founders Deepak Singh, Vikas Taneja and Prithviraj Singh was illegal and ordered their immediate release. The founders were arrested in May in connection with a money laundering probe linked to online gaming FIRs, with the ED alleging irregular transactions through Gameskraft’s real-money gaming platforms.

articles.title: Consumer Forum's Jurisdiction Not Ousted By Mere Existence Of Arbitration Clause : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that the existence of an arbitration clause does not prevent a consumer forum from hearing a dispute. This decision was made in a case where the National Consumer Dispute Redressal Commission (NCDRC) had referred the matter to arbitration. The court's ruling means that consumer forums can still adjudicate disputes even if there is an arbitration clause in the agreement.

articles.title: Why India, among largest economies, doesn’t rank in top 50 world’s most powerful passports
articles.summary: Despite India's robust economic growth, its passport ranks surprisingly low globally, hovering around the 80th position. This reflects a slower expansion of visa-free access compared to other nations. Factors like diplomatic ties, reciprocal agreements, and perceived security influence rankings, not just economic might. India's progress is gradual, requiring strategic diplomatic efforts and strengthened internal systems to boost international trust and improve its travel freedom.

articles.title: 7 new bullet train corridors approved: Centre steps up high-speed rail expansion across India
articles.summary: India is set to revolutionize its rail network with the approval of seven new bullet train corridors, promising significantly reduced travel times between major cities. Key routes like Mumbai-Ahmedabad will see journeys slashed to under two hours, while Bengaluru-Chennai will take just over an hour. This ambitious expansion, including the Delhi-Varanasi-Patna corridor, aims to boost connectivity and usher in an era of unprecedented railway development across the nation.

articles.title: RBI revises Kisan Credit Card norms, standardises crop season definition
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank has revamped the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme, standardizing crop season definitions for uniform loan sanctions and repayments. Effective January, new directions aim to streamline credit support for agriculture and allied activities. Banks must waive collateral for loans up to Rs 2 lakh, with exceptions for voluntary gold/silver pledges. For loans exceeding Rs 2 lakh, banks will set collateral based on their policy.

articles.title: Contempt Court Can't Test Legality Of Decision Taken In Compliance With Underlying Judicial Order: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has reiterated that the validity or correctness of an administrative decision taken in compliance with a court's direction cannot be examined in contempt proceedings. Justice Tejas Karia made the observation while disposing of a contempt petition filed by a college, which alleged wilful disobedience by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE) of earlier directions requiring it to decide the college's long-pending application for shifting its B.Ed. college premises.

articles.title: UPERC Clears 20 MW Floating Solar Project in Gorakhpur
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission (UPERC) has allowed the development and commissioning of a 20 MW floating solar power project at Chilwa Tal in the state’s Gorakhpur district through a cost-plus tariff mechanism and permitted Uttar Pradesh Power Corporation (UPPCL) to procure power for the project.The solar power project is to be established by Coal India (CIL).

articles.title: UP Invests ₹5,400 Crore In Green Energy Corridor-II To Support 22 GW Solar Ambitions
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh is advancing as a renewable energy hub with a ₹5,400 crore investment in the Green Energy Corridor-II project. This initiative will enhance power transmission infrastructure to achieve 22,000 MW of solar capacity. The project focuses on Bundelkhand's solar potential and encourages rooftop solar growth, aiming for a sustainable energy ecosystem. articles.summary: The Gujarat Electricity Regulatory Commission is mediating a dispute between Carysil Limited and PGVCL over the delay in commissioning a solar power project and the encashment of a ₹26.40 lakh bank guarantee. articles.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
articles.summary: Iran has stated it placed naval mines in the Strait of Hormuz to deter independent shipping traffic. The Strait is a vital route for global oil shipments, and mines pose a serious risk to maritime safety. The situation raises concerns about potential disruptions to energy supplies and regional stability, which could impact India's oil imports and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

articles.title: Lower oil prices open door for MPC to time policy normalisation: ICICI Research
articles.summary: Falling oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions have improved India's inflation outlook, giving the Monetary Policy Committee room to decide on policy normalization timing. While concerns about food and fuel prices were noted, underlying inflation remains stable. Domestic growth shows resilience, though export and rainfall risks persist. The MPC may lower inflation forecasts if oil prices remain subdued, potentially reducing near-term rate hike odds.
articles.raw_summary: Falling oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions have improved India's inflation outlook, giving the Monetary Policy Committee room to decide on policy normalization timing. The MPC may lower inflation forecasts if oil prices remain subdued, potentially reducing near-term rate hike odds.

articles.title: What is Kali365? FBI warns Telegram-based phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 users
articles.summary: The FBI has warned about a phishing service called Kali365 that is targeting Microsoft 365 users. This has implications for cybersecurity and data protection, but it does not have any direct policy implications for India.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh govt inks deal with Canva for visual communication in govt schools
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government has partnered with Canva to improve visual communication in government schools. This deal will benefit teachers and Education Department officials. The partnership aims to enhance the educational experience for students.
articles.raw_summary: The deal was struck on June 18 between the Education Department, Samagra Siksha and Canva, which will also benefit teachers and Education Department officials.

articles.title: ED case against five Bengaluru-based entities for 'unauthorised' cross-border crypto transactions
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has initiated a FEMA probe into five companies located in Bengaluru for their alleged involvement in unauthorized cross-border transactions amounting to Rs 2,500 crore through cryptocurrency. Investigations uncovered significant regulatory evasion via online ads promoting immediate fiat-to-crypto swaps and the establishment of shell companies. Consequently, the ED has frozen bank accounts totaling around Rs 6 crore.

Consequently, the ED has frozen bank accounts totaling around Rs 6 crore.

articles.title: RMZ to ramp up data center capacity with $35 billion push, exec says
articles.summary: RMZ Infrastructure plans to increase its data center capacity by 1 gigawatt, which is currently at 250 megawatts. This expansion may lead to increased energy consumption and potential policy implications for infrastructure development in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Bengaluru-headquartered company, which currently has 250 megawatts of capacity, is in the final stages of discussions for three data center ‌projects that ⁠would take ⁠its total capacity to more than 1 gigawatt, Deepak Chhabria, president of RMZ Infrastructure, told Reuters ​in an interview on Thursday.

articles.title: Meta in India doing what others can’t: Infrastructure head Santosh Janardhan
articles.summary: Meta Platforms is set to build significant AI data centre capacity in India. The company is already working on a large data centre in Jamnagar with Reliance Industries. Meta's Bengaluru engineering hub plays a crucial role in developing proprietary silicon, said Santosh Janardhan global head of infrastructure at Meta Platforms.

articles.title: International Finance Corporation announces $371 million investment in data centre operator Sify
articles.summary: The International Finance Corporation is investing $371 million in Sify Technologies. This funding will develop two new data centres in Navi Mumbai and Chennai. These facilities will be AI-ready and energy-efficient. The investment supports India's growing digital needs and expansion of its data centre capacity. This move is set to boost India's digital transformation and infrastructure.

articles.summary: At Vivatech 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasized the necessity of democratizing technology for sustainable development, describing AI as 'All Inclusive' for the nation. With India setting a benchmark in digital transactions, he showcased the country's commitment to utilizing technology for enhancing financial inclusion and educational access.

articles.title: Dhan parent Raise Securities posts Rs 905 crore revenue, profit down 20% in FY26
articles.summary: The company had reported net operating income of Rs 795 crore and a profit after tax of Rs 408 crore in FY25. The rating agency attributed the increase in operating expenses in FY26 to elevated marketing spend, team expansion, and one-off non-recurring expenses.

articles.title: Fintechs bet big on corporate bonds as retail participation surges
articles.summary: Retail investors are increasingly participating in corporate bonds, with monthly investments surging to Rs 1,500-2,000 crore. This growth is driven by increased advertising, investor education, and a wider range of issuers. Fintech platforms are actively expanding their offerings in this segment, recognizing bonds as a significant growth opportunity.

articles.title: PE firms join GCC boom, push portfolio companies to set up captive centres
articles.summary: Private equity-backed firms are increasingly establishing Global Capability Centres (GCCs) in India, moving beyond cost savings to build AI, engineering, and product capabilities. This trend signifies a shift from large corporations to mid-market companies leveraging GCCs as a strategic value-creation tool for portfolio companies.

articles.title: India key to Mercedes’ technology stack, autonomous-driving ambitions: CTO Jörg Burzer
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz is significantly leveraging its Indian R D center in Bengaluru for its biggest software transformation to date, MB.OS, which will power future S-Class models and other vehicles. This proprietary operating system, designed with AI integration, is crucial for the automaker's shift towards software-defined platforms and autonomous driving ambitions.

articles.title: Tata Electronics onboards Malaysian cleanroom specialist IAQ Group for Dholera fab
articles.summary: Tata Electronics has partnered with IAQ Group to ensure its upcoming semiconductor fab in Dholera meets stringent cleanroom standards. This collaboration aims to ensure timely execution and de-risk the cleanroom readiness bottleneck. The partnership is crucial for the facility's development.

IAQ Group's expertise will be crucial for timely execution and de-risking the cleanroom readiness bottleneck.

articles.title: Electronics now third largest export category, next target second rank: Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: India's electronics exports are surging, now its third-largest category, with ambitions to become second. The nation's electronics manufacturing sector is valued at Rs 13 lakh crore. The minister was speaking at the inauguration of American electronics manufacturing services firm Jabil’s new advanced facility in Pune.

articles.title: Jio denies any role in BGP hijacking after Telegram CEO Pavel Durov allegations
articles.summary: Reliance Jio has denied allegations of sabotaging Telegram access outside India. Telegram CEO Pavel Durov claimed BGP hijacking was used. Experts note ambiguity in Durov's statements, with one mentioning Reliance Communications (AS18101) and a snapshot showing the same. Reliance Jio Infocomm (AS55836) was not named in the snapshot. Jio states it follows global best practices for network operations.

articles.title: Weak start to monsoon seen clouding Kharif season outlook: Report
articles.summary: India's monsoon has begun with a significant rainfall deficit, impacting kharif sowing, particularly for pulses and cotton. While reservoir levels offer a buffer, a prolonged dry spell could raise food prices and strain rural economies. Experts emphasize close monitoring, as the July-August period will be crucial for crop prospects and overall economic recovery.

Experts emphasize close monitoring, as the July-August period will be crucial for crop prospects and overall economic recovery.

articles.title: India on track for $1 trillion exports in FY27: Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India is poised to hit its ambitious $1 trillion export goal by FY27, despite ongoing West Asia challenges, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He expressed strong confidence, citing progress and the impact of free trade agreements and improved business ease. Discussions with the US on a trade pact are finalized, awaiting India's tariff advantage over rivals for implementation.

articles.title: India, US trade deal can't be implemented until we have 'competitive advantage': Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal on Saturday said India cannot implement the proposed trade agreement with the United States until it secures a competitive advantage over competing nations, indicating that tariff-related issues remain the final hurdle in the negotiations.

articles.title: El Nino Impact: Milk prices likely to go up, again
articles.summary: El Nino is expected to lead to poor rainfall, which may cause farmers to reduce cattle numbers and lower milk output, potentially pushing up milk prices.
articles.raw_summary: Poor rainfall typically leads farmers to reduce cattle numbers because of shortages of fodder and water, lowering milk output and pushing up prices.

articles.title: Rs 19,700-crore carbon capture scheme gets nod
articles.summary: The carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) scheme is expected to attract investments of about ₹37,500 crore and create carbon-capture capacity of 7 million tonnes a year, they said. The next step is to get the approval of the Union Cabinet.

articles.title: PLI, duty incentives soon for Lithium, Nickel processing units in India
articles.summary: The proposed scheme, which is close to securing approvals, will offer production-linked incentives and waive import duties on capital goods needed to establish processing facilities, a senior government official told ET.
articles.raw_summary: The proposed scheme, which is close to securing approvals, will offer production-linked incentives and waive import duties on capital goods needed to establish processing facilities, a senior government official told ET.

articles.title: India’s first export of fresh Uttarakhand Litchi to Italy marks entry into European market
articles.summary: Uttarakhand's famed Dehradun litchis have officially entered the European market with the first export consignment reaching Italy. This significant move, facilitated by APEDA, saw one metric tonne of the sweet, aromatic fruit dispatched on June 18, 2026. The initiative promises better returns for local farmers, who are now seeing prices around 25% higher than domestic rates, boosting the region's agricultural economy.

articles.title: India, Uzbekistan aim to double bilateral trade in 3 years
articles.summary: India and Uzbekistan are set to significantly boost bilateral trade, aiming to double it within three years. Discussions at the recent Intergovernmental Commission focused on expanding exports of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, auto, and machinery. Both nations also explored cooperation in customs, payment infrastructure, agriculture, ICT, and energy, with a particular emphasis on securing critical minerals for India's digital economy.

articles.title: Seven-fold rise in funds under externally aided projects in northeast under Modi government: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Chairing a conference on "Leveraging externally aided projects in the northeastern states" here, she said that assistance under the externally aided projects (EAP) in the northeastern region has seen a seven-fold rise under the Narendra Modi government.

articles.title: RBI MPC member Nagesh Kumar flags import dependence, Gulf remittances, El Nino risks in minutes
articles.summary: India faces significant economic risks from the West Asia conflict, including potential disruptions to oil and fertilizer imports via the Strait of Hormuz and reduced remittances from the Gulf. El Niño-induced monsoon failures also pose a threat to agriculture. Despite these vulnerabilities, the nation's strong foreign exchange reserves and fiscal space offer some resilience. Growth projections have been tempered, highlighting the need for careful economic management.

articles.title: India’s forex reserves fall $9.99 billion to $671.63 billion as of June 12
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a dip of nearly $10 billion to $671.63 billion in the week ending June 12, 2026. This decline was primarily driven by a significant drop in gold reserves, despite a rise in foreign currency assets. The nation still holds a substantial forex buffer, crucial for economic stability and currency support amidst market fluctuations.

articles.title: Ample world inventories may soften El Nino food supply shock
articles.summary: A potential super El Niño may disrupt global weather patterns and affect agricultural output. Large existing food inventories and strong harvests in key producing regions may soften the impact. This could have implications for India's food security and agricultural policies.
articles.raw_summary: A potential super El Niño is expected to disrupt global weather patterns and threaten agricultural output in the coming months, but large existing food inventories and strong harvests in key producing regions may soften the impact.

articles.title: Despite West Asia crisis, net direct tax mopup grows 15% to Rs 5.2 lakh crore
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant rise of over 14 percent. This growth is driven by strong advance tax payments from corporations. This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.

This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.

articles.title: EPF interest rate retained at 8.25% for FY26
articles.summary: The EPF interest rate for 2025-26 has been set at 8.25%. This rate remains unchanged for the third year. Over 70 million EPF members will see the interest credited by month-end.
articles.raw_summary: Employees' Provident Fund interest rate for 2025-26 is set at 8.25 percent. This rate remains unchanged for the third year. Over 70 million EPF members will see the interest credited by month-end. EPFO is also introducing UPI-based withdrawals for faster access to funds.

articles.title: Peace in West Asia expected to boost order book of Indian exporters: Industry body FIEO
articles.summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.

articles.title: India's net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to Rs 5.21 lakh crore till June 17
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant jump. Net collections reached Rs 5.21 lakh crore by June 17, marking a 14.64% increase. This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

articles.title: Will continue to support leveraging trade pact provisions, says UK India Business Council
articles.summary: India and the UK are set to implement their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement on July 15. The UK India Business Council will assist its members in understanding and utilizing the pact's benefits. This landmark deal aims to boost trade and investment between the two nations. Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

articles.title: 'Fastest we've ever put a trade deal into force': British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron hails India-UK trade deal
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will take effect on July 15, 2026, marking the fastest trade deal implementation between the two nations. This historic agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $100-$120 billion by 2030, offering significant economic benefits across sectors like textiles, footwear, and cars.

articles.title: India weighs extending 40-product import duty exemption beyond June 30 amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The government will decide on extending import duty exemptions for 40 products after June 30. This decision hinges on the evolving situation in West Asia and its impact on cargo movement. Revenue implications for the government are also a key consideration. The current exemption was put in place to support domestic industries facing supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: El Nino fear pushes farmers in Srikakulam and Vizianagaram towards water-saving paddy methods
articles.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are adopting water-saving rice farming techniques to mitigate the effects of potential El Nino. This shift aims to reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is promoting these changes to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

articles.title: UKIBC hails India-UK trade pact rollout from July 15, says CETA to unlock new growth opportunities
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement, CETA, will become effective on July 15, 2026. This landmark deal opens new avenues for trade and investment between India and the United Kingdom. Businesses are encouraged to prepare for its implementation. The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

articles.title: Govt ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
articles.summary: The government has approved an 8.25% interest rate on Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) deposits for FY26, clearing the proposal recommended by the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's (EPFO) Central Board of Trustees in March. The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

articles.summary: India is in talks with the World Bank and Asian Development Bank for substantial funding. This financial support aims to boost urban infrastructure and create jobs. The loans will help New Delhi manage its budget after increased spending on subsidies. The government plans to upgrade infrastructure and urban renewal projects.

articles.summary: Scientific innovations and integrated farming systems are combating land degradation and enhancing climate resilience, benefiting millions of farmers and contributing to national development goals.

articles.title: US buyers keen to stitch deals with Indian apparel exporters
articles.summary: Indian apparel exporters face pressure as US buyers demand discounts of up to 10%. This comes as India's readymade garment exports have declined for six consecutive months, largely due to weaker demand in the United States. Competitors like Vietnam and Indonesia are gaining ground.

articles.title: Govt readies farm safety net with banks, insurers as El Nino looms
articles.summary: The Indian government is taking steps to protect rural families from the effects of El Nino on farming. This includes improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be used to support these efforts.
articles.raw_summary: The government is enhancing financial safety nets for rural families. This initiative aims to cushion the impact of El Nino on farming. Efforts include improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be utilized. The Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana is also under review for broader coverage.

articles.title: RBI temporarily withdraws rate caps on FCNR(B), NRE deposits till Sept 30
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has removed interest rate caps on specific non-resident deposits. This move grants banks more freedom to attract foreign funds. The changes are effective immediately and will continue until September 2026. Banks can now offer higher rates on FCNR(B) and NRE deposits. This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

articles.title: Father’s Day: The financial legacy children truly inherit
articles.summary: Indian fathers are redefining legacy, moving beyond just assets to financial behaviour. The rise of micro-investing and digital platforms allows for consistent, small-scale participation, making wealth creation an everyday habit. This shift, driven by accessibility and automation, teaches children valuable lessons in discipline and long-term planning, shaping a more enduring inheritance than mere wealth.

articles.title: Earnings of OMCs seen weak as Q1FY27 under-recoveries bite: Report
articles.summary: Oil companies in India are expected to face challenges due to under-recoveries in Q1FY27. This is likely to affect their profit margins. The government's possible retraction of excise duty cuts could further exacerbate the situation.
articles.raw_summary: Oil companies are projected to experience ongoing challenges through FY27, primarily due to anticipated under-recoveries in Q1FY27, with LPG losses being a considerable issue. Although recent declines in crude prices provide short-term relief, ongoing market volatility and required inventory adjustments are expected to squeeze profit margins. A notable threat includes the government's possible retraction of excise duty cuts to mitigate revenue shortfalls.

articles.title: The yoga of asset allocation: Why stability matters more than ever
articles.summary: Investors need stability in today's volatile markets. Asset allocation, including bonds, can help mitigate sharp market movements and ensure long-term wealth creation. This is crucial for investors looking to grow their wealth.
articles.raw_summary: In today's volatile markets, investors need stability more than ever, drawing parallels from yoga's emphasis on balance. Asset allocation, particularly the inclusion of bonds, is crucial for mitigating sharp market movements and ensuring long-term wealth creation. While equities offer growth, bonds provide predictable returns and act as a stabilizing force, helping portfolios withstand global shocks and domestic surprises.

articles.title: Warsh's gamble: A quieter Federal Reserve could mean volatile markets, higher rates
articles.summary: The new Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, is reducing the central bank's communication, which may lead to market volatility and higher borrowing costs. This change could have implications for the global economy, including India. Analysts warn that this move may contrast with previous chairs' efforts to stabilize markets and lower rates.
articles.raw_summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is significantly reducing the central bank's communication, particularly "forward guidance" on interest rates. This move, aimed at making markets less reliant on Fed signals, has already caused market volatility. Analysts warn this could lead to higher borrowing costs for consumers and businesses, contrasting with previous chairs who used guidance to stabilize markets and lower rates.

articles.title: Jio Platforms plans $3 billion debt reduction from IPO proceeds
articles.summary: Jio Platforms plans to use a portion of its IPO proceeds to repay nearly $3 billion in external commercial borrowings. This move aims to reduce debt and interest expenses, allowing the company to pursue strategic growth in areas like 5G, broadband, AI, and cloud services.
articles.raw_summary: Jio Platforms is gearing up for its IPO, planning to allocate a significant portion of the funds to repay nearly $3 billion in external commercial borrowings for its telecom arm. This move aims to slash debt, reduce interest expenses, and bolster the company's financial standing. The deleveraging will empower Jio to pursue strategic growth in areas like 5G, broadband, AI, and cloud services, positioning it for future investments and opportunities.

articles.title: Monsoon risk, younger investor base, and skewed trading activity define NSE outlook: NSE data
articles.summary: India's 2026 macro outlook will hinge on monsoon performance, while the investor base continues to get younger and more geographically dispersed, and trading activity remains heavily concentrated among large investors, NSE data showed in its latest report.
articles.raw_summary: India's 2026 macro outlook will hinge on monsoon performance, while the investor base continues to get younger and more geographically dispersed, and trading activity remains heavily concentrated among large investors, NSE data showed in its latest report.

articles.summary: BSE subsidiary ICCL has adopted a new brand identity as BSE Clearing, marking a significant milestone in its alignment with parent entity BSE Ltd. This move reinforces its role within the broader BSE ecosystem. The transition is a regulatory action that strengthens the alignment between the two entities.

articles.title: Sebi reinstates open market buybacks via exchanges
articles.summary: Sebi has greenlit buyback flexibility for companies, reintroducing open market options via exchanges from August 1. Mutual funds gain relaxed borrowing norms for liquidity, and AIFs will see faster fundraising. These moves aim to boost capital management for listed firms, with buybacks needing completion within 66 days and 40% funds deployed early. Merchant banker appointments are now optional, lowering costs.

articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Assembly unanimously adopted a resolution against Karnataka's Mekedatu dam. Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay piloted the resolution, which was backed by opposition parties including Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin and AIADMK chief Edappadi K Palaniswami. The resolution is a significant development in the nearly 13-year-long dispute between the two states.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu finally admits its biggest debt problem. But can it afford the solution?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's latest fiscal White Paper identifies the power sector as the state's biggest source of financial risk, with electricity utilities carrying debt of ₹2.47 lakh crore and accumulated losses of ₹1.82 lakh crore. While recent assessments suggest the financial health of the state's distribution utility has improved, the key question is whether the turnaround is sustainable—or whether the burden has simply shifted from TNEB's balance sheet to the state's own finances.

articles.title: NCB launches voluntary code for chemical industry to curb diversion of precursor chemicals

articles.title: Ladakh LG approves constitution of Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society
articles.summary: The LG of Ladakh has approved the constitution of the Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society. The society will be chaired by the LG, with several officials as ex-officio members. This move aims to conserve the snow leopard and its habitat in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The LG of Ladakh will be the chairman of the society, while the chief secretary, Ladakh MP, chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Leh, the chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Kargil, and the principal secretary, Department of Forest, Ecology and Environment, will be the ex-officio members, an official spokesperson said.

articles.title: A single 3.3 kg mango from Madhya Pradesh sold for ₹3,800 — why is it so expensive?
articles.summary: A rare and expensive mango variety from Madhya Pradesh has been sold for ₹3,800. The Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. Its limited availability and short harvest window contribute to its high price.
articles.raw_summary: Grown in limited quantities in Alirajpur’s Katthiwada region, the Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. The variety attracts buyers every season due to its jumbo weight, short harvest window and limited availability.

This move aims to clarify the legal provisions governing Aadhaar's use.

Northwest India has been the only bright spot, receiving 13% more rainfall than normal.

articles.title: 'We are all grateful to PM Modi': Top ministers, CMs perform yoga across India
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi led International Yoga Day celebrations in Kolkata, emphasizing "Yoga for Healthy Ageing" and its role in maintaining physical and mental well-being. Across India, Union ministers and chief ministers participated in mass yoga sessions, highlighting yoga's global reach and its contribution to a healthier, more vibrant nation. The day marked a collective global celebration of yoga's benefits.

articles.title: FSSAI turns lens on 'natural', 'healthy' labels
articles.summary: India's food regulator, FSSAI, is cracking down on misleading health claims on food and supplement labels. Products marketed as "natural," "healthy," or "no added sugar" are under scrutiny for potentially deceiving consumers. This intensified enforcement follows rising complaints and social media attention, aiming to ensure transparency and protect public health by urging consumers to look beyond catchy marketing phrases.

articles.title: At Lahore's 'Eton', Pakistani alum pays tribute to Indian dost
articles.summary: A classroom at Lahore's Aitchison College, once attended by leaders from undivided Punjab, now bridges India and Pakistan. A tribute to former Punjab CM Harcharan Singh Brar, funded by his 100-year-old schoolmate Syed Babar Ali, highlights enduring friendships forged before Partition. This initiative is part of a larger effort to remember the institution's pre-1947 past, honouring students and teachers scattered by history.

This initiative is part of a larger effort to remember the institution's pre-1947 past, honouring students and teachers scattered by history.

articles.title: Digital marking: CBSE to consult stakeholders
articles.summary: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) will consult students, teachers, and parents before deciding on the continuation of its on-screen marking system for Class XII exams and potential expansion to Class X. The review aims to address gaps exposed during recent exams and rebuild trust. The CBSE's decision will impact the education sector in India.
articles.raw_summary: CBSE's on-screen marking system for Class XII exams faces an uncertain future. The board will consult students, teachers, and parents before deciding on its continuation for 2027 and potential expansion to Class X. This review follows complaints about the system's debut, including scanning issues and unevaluated answers, and aims to address gaps exposed during recent exams and rebuild trust.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel rates may ease as cheaper crude arrives, says oil minister Puri
articles.summary: Petrol and diesel prices may soon see a dip as India receives cheaper crude oil, Union Minister Hardeep Singh Puri indicated. He defended domestic fuel pricing, stating increases have been minimal globally despite market volatility. The government has absorbed significant costs through excise duty cuts, shielding consumers from the full impact of rising international rates. Puri also highlighted Sonbhadra's development progress.

He defended domestic fuel pricing, stating increases have been minimal globally despite market volatility. The government has absorbed significant costs through excise duty cuts, shielding consumers from the full impact of rising international rates. Puri also highlighted Sonbhadra's development progress.

articles.title: 3 Indian-flagged oil tankers cross Strait of Hormuz, head home with 94 crew members
articles.summary: Three Indian oil tankers, carrying over 8.6 lakh metric tonnes of cargo and 94 crew members, have successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz and are heading home. This safe passage follows a US decision to lift its blockade on Iran, allowing resumed movement through the vital waterway. The government assures continued efforts to protect India's maritime and energy interests, with the vessels expected to reach Indian ports next week.

The government assures continued efforts to protect India's maritime and energy interests, with the vessels expected to reach Indian ports next week.

articles.title: Monsoon, El Niño and market trends: NSE highlights key risks for India’s 2026 economy
articles.summary: India's 2026 economic outlook hinges on monsoon performance, with El Niño posing a significant risk of deficient rainfall impacting agriculture. Meanwhile, the nation's equity investor base is rapidly expanding, becoming younger and more diverse, with a notable surge in participation from smaller cities. Despite this growth, trading activity remains heavily concentrated among a small group of large investors across all market segments.

Despite this growth, trading activity remains heavily concentrated among a small group of large investors across all market segments.

articles.title: Milk prices may rise again by July as El Niño threatens fodder supply
articles.summary: Dairy consumers in India may face another price hike due to potential fodder shortages caused by El Niño. This could lead to a 3-4% increase in milk prices by July or August. The Maharashtra government has urged farmers to boost fodder cultivation to mitigate the impact.
articles.raw_summary: Dairy consumers might face another price hike by July or August, with industry experts warning of a potential 3-4% increase. This follows a recent 2-3% rise in May. Concerns are mounting over El Niño potentially worsening fodder shortages and impacting milk production, prompting the Maharashtra government to urge farmers to boost fodder cultivation. Major dairies are closely monitoring the situation.

articles.title: Middle East conflict pushes India to rethink LPG imports as OMCs continued to absorb price shocks
articles.summary: India significantly diversified its LPG imports, tapping the US, Iran, and other nations to reduce Middle East dependence amid regional crisis. While state-run retailers absorbed price hikes, domestic consumption dipped due to tighter supplies and rising costs. The move, though ensuring continuity, highlighted ongoing import reliance and the need for a broader sourcing strategy to mitigate future geopolitical risks and market volatility.
articles.raw_summary: India significantly diversified its LPG imports, tapping the US, Iran, and other nations to reduce Middle East dependence amid regional crisis. The move, though ensuring continuity, highlighted ongoing import reliance and the need for a broader sourcing strategy to mitigate future geopolitical risks and market volatility.

articles.title: Crude comeback: 20 million barrels leave Iran port after peace breakthrough
articles.summary: Iran's oil exports have increased after a peace deal with Washington. However, the delay of permanent peace talks may impact the long-term outlook. This development coincides with new maritime regulations for the Strait of Hormuz.
articles.raw_summary: Iran's oil exports have surged following a peace deal with Washington, with 11 tankers carrying 20 million barrels departing the Gulf of Oman. This increase coincides with new maritime regulations for the Strait of Hormuz. However, the delay of permanent peace talks due to regional clashes casts a shadow on the long-term outlook, with lighter traffic observed on Friday.

articles.title: Accenture outlook cut sparks fresh fears over IT recovery
articles.summary: Global tech spending concerns have intensified as Accenture lowered its FY26 revenue growth forecast to 3-4%. This outlook, despite a resilient third quarter, signals potential prolonged weak demand for Indian IT services firms, possibly extending into FY27. Analysts suggest this weakness at a leading IT player negatively impacts demand prospects for India's top IT companies, hinting at a slower start to the next fiscal year.

articles.title: Rupee ends higher for second day, settles at 94.33 as trade deal hopes offset oil concerns
articles.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened for a second day, closing at 94.33 against the US dollar, buoyed by positive India-US trade talks. Hopes for an interim trade agreement between the two nations are rising, with negotiators urged to expedite efforts. However, global uncertainties and a stronger dollar limited the rupee's gains. Analysts anticipate continued inflows supporting the local currency.

articles.summary: Jio Platforms, a major Indian telecom company, has revealed potential risks and challenges ahead of its IPO. These include spectrum renewal challenges, cybersecurity threats, and evolving AI and data privacy regulations. These risks may impact the company's future growth and profitability.

These disclosures highlight potential hurdles to its future growth and profitability as it prepares for its market debut.

articles.title: Kisan Credit Card rules revised: RBI standardises crop season norms, retains collateral-free loan limit
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has revamped the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) framework, standardizing crop season definitions and aligning them with banking asset-classification norms. Effective January 2027, these changes aim to simplify farm loan processes and ensure timely credit for agriculture and allied activities. Collateral-free lending up to Rs 2 lakh per borrower remains, with flexibility for loans up to Rs 3 lakh under specific arrangements.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | What a strong El Nino could mean for India
articles.summary: One of the strongest El Nino events in decades is forecast to intensify through the year, raising the risk of a weak monsoon in India. From crop output and food prices to water supplies and electricity generation, Mint explains the potential impact.

articles.title: Mint Explainer| What would it take for India to create a SpaceX?
articles.summary: SpaceX’s $2.5 trillion valuation is driven by its reusable rocket technology, which slashed launch costs and dominates the global space market. India’s startups, Skyroot and Agnikul, are targeting small-satellite launches, but need capital, scale and consistent execution to build a success story.

articles.title: Summer Solstice 2026: What is the longest day of the year and why does it happen?
